task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: An image is loaded in GIMP
task_completion.visual_evidence: A photograph is visible in the main GIMP canvas.

task_completion.description: The Color Temperature dialog is open
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Color Temperature' dialog box is visible on screen (typically accessed via Colors -> Color Temperature).

task_completion.description: The temperature is adjusted to a warmer tone in the dialog
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Color Temperature dialog, the 'Intended temperature' value is increased or the slider is moved towards the right (warmer/yellow/red side).

task_completion.description: The warmer color temperature is applied to the image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Color Temperature dialog is closed or the preview is active, and the image in the main canvas visually appears warmer (more yellow/orange) compared to its initial state.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to adjust the color temperature
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the GIMP graphical interface, not a terminal or command-line image processing tool.

integrity.description: The agent used the Color Temperature tool
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Color Temperature' dialog is used, rather than painting a yellow/orange layer or using unrelated color tools to fake the effect.

integrity.description: The agent modified the original image
integrity.visual_evidence: The original photograph is the one being edited, not a different image or a pre-edited file opened from elsewhere.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP 2.10. The Color Temperature tool is located in the Colors menu. The primary image is a 1000x666px JPEG photograph. A warmer tone means the image should visually shift towards yellow/orange/red. The agent must use the Color Temperature tool to achieve this effect.
